title: quercus durata var. durata
intro: Quercus durata var. durata, also known as the golden cup oak, is a variety of tree that belongs to the family Fagaceae, characterized by its golden-hued acorns and distinctive leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The golden cup oak is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark, leaves, and acorns are commonly used in herbal remedies. Furthermore, the plant is valued for its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, improve immune function, and promote overall well-being.
